world.title: Five Indians among 12 injured by Iranian missile debris in Abu Dhabi
world.description: Middle East News: At least 12 people, including five Indian nationals, were injured in Abu Dhabi on Friday after falling debris landed following the interception of an .

world.title: Trump pick Frank Garcia set for top Africa role
world.description: Senate aides told Semafor that the body is expected to vote soon on Frank Garcia’s confirmation to be Assistant Secretary of State for African Affairs.

world.title: Africa ‘needs capital’ to reap AI boom
world.description: African countries should mobilize more capital from domestic markets to adapt to the global shift in economic development sparked by AI, says a new UN report.

world.title: 'Fraud Czar': Trump gives JD Vance new role; 'Blue States' in crosshairs
world.description: US News: US President Donald Trump on Friday said that his deputy JD Vance will focus on investigating fraud in democratic-run states in his new role as \"Fraud.

world.title: Ghana to grant free visas to all Africans from May 25 - President Mahama announces
world.description: Ghana is set to open its borders further to the continent, with President John Dramani Mahama announcing the introduction of a free visa regime for all African nationals as part of efforts to deepen regional integration and economic cooperation.

world.title: Israel's PM says 70% of Iran's steel production capacity destroyed, hindering weapons production
world.description: Friday began with Israel saying it was under attack by a new barrage of missiles from Iran, as President Donald Trump warned the United States had yet to begin "destroying what's left" of the Islamic republic's infrastructure.

world.title: Trump proposes $1.5 trillion defense spending, 10% cut to domestic programs to fuel Iran war effort
world.description: Discretionary nondefense spending would be cut 10%, by about $73 billion, according to fact sheets circulated by the White House in advance of the annual budget release Friday.

world.title: Africa’s $35bn sports tourism opportunity: growth accelerates alongside traditional travel sectors
world.description:
Africa’s sports economy could expand from $12bn to $30-35bn over the next decade
Tourism contributes up to 8% of GDP across the continent,

business.title: The Iran War Will Disrupt the Digital Order and Redefine Sovereignty
business.description: S. Yash Kalash writes that the Iran conflict reveals a war across infrastructure and supply chains, accelerating a fragmented global technological order.

business.title: Gold: The three-year rally may not be over yet
business.description: The Middle East conflict is weighing on gold prices amid expectations that central banks will raise interest rates to address rising inflation driven by oil prices. This seems like a knee-jerk reaction, as this is precisely how central banks acted in 2022.

business.title: 3.1 million bottles of eye drops sold at Walgreens, CVS and more are recalled. Here's what to know.
business.description: The eye drops — sold under multiple brands — have been recalled over concerns about sterility, according to the FDA.

science.title: James Webb Space Telescope Spots Huge Black Holes Where They Shouldn't Be, Adding to the Mystery of "Little Red Dots"
science.description: James Webb Space Telescope observations have revealed unexpectedly overmassive supermassive black holes at the hearts of a pair of galaxies.

science.title: Artemis II crew member becomes ‘space plumber’ to fix blocked toilet

science.title: The secrets of black holes and the Higgs mass could be hidden in a 7-dimensional geometry
science.description: One of the greatest mysteries of modern physics, the "black hole information paradox," might have finally found an elegant solution, and the answer could also reveal the origins of the mass of fundamental particles.

science.title: Lord of the Half-Rings: The White Dwarfs 'Gandalf' and 'Moon-Sized' Represent a New Class of Stellar Objects
science.description: Gandalf and Moon-Sized are a new class of stellar remnants characterized by five strange parameters, making them unique from other objects.

science.title: Scientists Just Discovered There’s Actually Something Faster than the Speed of Light
science.description: Scientists experimentally confirmed what we’ve known to be true for half a century—darkness can “travel” faster than light.

science.title: How NASA designed the Artemis II space suits for a worst-case scenario
science.description: For its return to the moon, NASA designed a suit that can keep astronauts alive for six days should the Orion spaceship fail.
